messages.content: We need to pick a database. I'm leaning Postgres because we already have ops experience with it. MongoDB would mean ramping up a new operational stack.

Postgres is the right call. You get JSONB for document-shaped data, full-text search, and your team already has runbooks. MongoDB's main wins (horizontal sharding) aren't load-bearing for your traffic profile.
Agreed. Let's go Postgres. I'll update the architecture doc.
Decision recorded: Postgres over MongoDB. Reason: existing ops expertise plus JSONB covers the document use case.
